reported_entities.name: Samsung
reported_entities.claim: Samsung Electronics uses MediaTek Dimensity processors in some smartphone models.

reported_entities.name: Xiaomi
reported_entities.claim: Xiaomi has shipped flagship phones (e.g. Redmi K50, Xiaomi 17 Ultra with Dimensity 9500) using MediaTek Dimensity chipsets.

reported_entities.name: Redmi
reported_entities.claim: Redmi (Xiaomi sub-brand) K50 debuted with the MediaTek Dimensity 9000 chipset; Redmi K series occasionally uses Dimensity chips.

reported_entities.name: Poco
reported_entities.claim: POCO Mobiles (Xiaomi sub-brand) has models available in India powered by MediaTek Dimensity chipsets.

reported_entities.name: Oppo
reported_entities.claim: Oppo (Find X series, Find X8 Ultra) has used MediaTek Dimensity flagship chipsets including the Dimensity 9000 and 9500.

reported_entities.name: Vivo
reported_entities.claim: Vivo has released premium phones (e.g. X200 Pro) using MediaTek Dimensity 9000/9500 series chipsets.

reported_entities.name: iQOO
reported_entities.claim: iQOO (Vivo sub-brand) is reported to be preparing a flagship smartphone using the MediaTek Dimensity 9500 chipset.

reported_entities.name: Realme
reported_entities.claim: Realme's GT 8 Pro flagship uses the MediaTek Dimensity 9500 chipset.

reported_entities.name: Honor
reported_entities.claim: Honor's Magic 8 Pro flagship uses the MediaTek Dimensity 9500 chipset; Honor also confirmed earlier Dimensity 9000 flagships.

reported_entities.name: Motorola
reported_entities.claim: Motorola's Razr line (including the base Razr) uses MediaTek Dimensity chipsets such as the Dimensity 7450X.

reported_entities.name: Nothing
reported_entities.claim: Nothing Phone (2a) is powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 7200 Pro; later Nothing models used Dimensity 7300 Pro / 7350 Pro.

reported_entities.name: Asus
reported_entities.claim: ASUS Mobiles has smartphone models available in India powered by MediaTek Dimensity chipsets.

reported_entities.name: Tecno
reported_entities.claim: Tecno Mobile uses MediaTek processors, including Dimensity chips, in its device lineup.

reported_entities.name: Infinix
reported_entities.claim: Infinix Mobile uses MediaTek processors, including Dimensity chips, in its device lineup.

reported_entities.name: Itel
reported_entities.claim: Itel Mobile uses MediaTek processors, including Dimensity chips, in its device lineup.

reported_entities.name: Red Magic (Nubia)
reported_entities.claim: Red Magic 11 Pro (Nubia's gaming phone sub-brand) uses the MediaTek Dimensity 9500 chipset.

notes: Web-search-only research (WebSearch tool, 3 queries used of 5 budget; no WebFetch or Bash used). Brands compiled from MediaTek press materials and tech news aggregating Dimensity-powered device announcements. Confidence varies: Samsung, Xiaomi/Redmi/Poco, Oppo, Vivo/iQOO, Realme, Honor, Motorola, Nothing, and Red Magic/Nubia are well-documented with specific model+chipset pairings. Tecno, Infinix, Itel, and Asus are supported by more general secondary-source claims (MediaTek partner lists / regional device listings) rather than a specific confirmed flagship model+chipset citation found within the query budget.
